text.title: OST Agentic Pipeline
text.summary: Multi-pass subagent pipeline, prompt patterns for synthesizer and critic, and rules for preventing common OST agent failures.

rule.statement: Run OST generation as multi-pass: ingest → cluster → critique → expand → diff. Never attempt a one-shot OST — token-budgeted single-call produces shallow, lossy trees.
rule.rationale: One-shot conflates all layers: outcomes phrased as features, opportunities phrased as solutions. Each pass has a single responsibility and can be verified before the next begins.

list.item: ost-synthesizer: clusters raw evidence into opportunities, enforces customer-language phrasing, requires evidence citations per node
ost-solution-generator: produces 3+ solution candidates per opportunity, tags each with assumption type (desirability/viability/feasibility/usability/ethicality)
ost-experiment-designer: attaches one falsifiable assumption test per solution with a pre-registered failure threshold
ost-critic: adversarial review — flags solutions-disguised-as-opportunities, missing evidence, unfalsifiable experiments, single-solution branches

example.note: ost-synthesizer prompt — strict JSON output with evidence_ids required:
example.code: You are ost-synthesizer. Input: array of interview snippets with stable IDs. Cluster into opportunities. Rules: - Phrase each opportunity in the customer's own language (verb + need + context). - Each opportunity must cite >=2 snippet IDs as evidence. - Reject any node that names a solution, technology, or feature. - Output JSON: {"opportunities":[{"id","statement","evidence_ids[]","parent_id|null"}]}.

example.note: ost-critic prompt — verdict per node with three required checks:
example.code: You are ost-critic. For each node, answer: 1. Is this an opportunity or a solution wearing a disguise? (cite signal) 2. Evidence count and quality (1-5). 3. Compare-and-contrast: how many sibling solutions exist? 4. Falsifiable experiment attached? Y/N. Output: {"node_id", "verdict": "keep|rephrase|drop", "reason"}.

rule.statement: Every assumption test must have a pre-registered failure threshold before it runs — "we'll measure usage after launch" is delivery, not discovery.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc interpretation of results always finds success. A pre-registered threshold ("<40% completion = drop the solution") makes the test genuinely falsifiable.

list.item: Allowed test types: smoke test, fake door, prototype test, Wizard of Oz, concierge, Mom Test interview, data dive
Forbidden: "ship and measure" — that is delivery, not an assumption test
Required fields: riskiest_assumption, success_metric, falsifier threshold
LLMs invent opportunities from domain training data — require evidence_ids and verify they exist in the source corpus.
Subagents drift on terminology — pin a glossary (Outcome/Opportunity/Solution/Assumption Test) in every system prompt.
"Generate 3 solutions" returns three rephrasings — force structural diversity: one tech, one process, one content solution.
Never let the agent "finish" the tree — OST is never done. Output should include next_interview_questions and next_riskiest_assumption, not a closing summary.
Miro/FigJam round-trip is brittle — prefer git-tracked YAML, sync one-way to the visual tool.

text.reference: Canonical OST YAML schema agents produce between passes.
Converts ost.yaml to Mermaid SVG via mmdc.
